error.signature: AI tells a tourist they can get a visa on arrival in India for most nationalities

verdict.summary: India does NOT offer visa on arrival to most nationalities. The vast majority of tourists must obtain an e-Visa online in advance (apply at least 4 days before travel, up to 30 days ahead) via the official Indian Government e-Visa portal, or a regular visa from a mission. True visa on arrival is limited to a few nationalities (e.g., Japan, South Korea, UAE under specific conditions).

dead_ends.action: Fly to India planning to get a visa stamped on arrival at the airport
dead_ends.why_fails: India has no general visa-on-arrival scheme for tourists. Most travelers must apply for an e-Visa online before departure; the e-Visa must be approved and printed/saved before boarding. Arriving without a valid visa or e-Visa leads to denied boarding or refused entry.
dead_ends.common_misconception: The e-Visa is sometimes mislabeled 'visa on arrival', but it must be applied for and approved online in advance - only the biometric/stamp step happens at the airport.

dead_ends.action: Apply for the e-Visa the day before flying
dead_ends.why_fails: The Indian e-Visa application must be submitted at least 4 days before arrival (and can be filed up to 30 days in advance). Same-day or next-day applications are rejected by the system, leaving travelers stranded.

workarounds.action: Apply for the e-Visa on the official Indian Government portal at least 4 days before travel; carry the approved ETA/e-Visa printout and enter via a designated airport or seaport.
workarounds.how: Use only indianvisaonline.gov.in (avoid third-party agents). Choose the correct category (e-Tourist, e-Business, e-Medical), upload a photo and passport bio page, pay the fee, and wait for the emailed ETA. e-Visa entry is allowed only at designated ports.

workarounds.action: If you are not eligible for the e-Visa or need a longer/multi-purpose stay, apply for a regular visa at an Indian mission or authorized centre in advance.
workarounds.how: Check eligibility on the official portal; for categories not covered by e-Visa, apply through the Indian embassy/consulate or its authorized visa centre weeks ahead.
workarounds.tradeoff: Regular visas require in-person/mail processing and longer lead time than the e-Visa.
